semantic.role: System overview document for ArchSpine, a documentation mirror and governance tool that synchronizes a structured atlas of documentation with the codebase, enabling automated consistency checks and knowledge views.
responsibilities: Defines the system vision for ArchSpine as a documentation mirror and governance layer.
Describes module orchestration and interaction between components.
Covers maintenance boundaries for the atlas, rules, scanning, and view generation.
Explains the roles of configuration, runtime, and synchronization mechanisms.
outOfScope: Detailed API reference or CLI command documentation.
Tutorials or how-to guides for specific workflows.
Implementation details of individual services or engines.
Third-party integration specifics beyond the MCP protocol.

English.purpose: This document explains the overarching vision and architectural orchestration of the ArchSpine system. It is designed to help developers, architects, and maintainers understand why ArchSpine exists, how its modules work together to maintain a living documentation mirror, and what boundaries govern its maintenance.
English.context_and_audience: The intended audience includes project leads, DevOps engineers, and AI agents responsible for configuring and extending the ArchSpine tool. It assumes familiarity with core concepts like documentation atlas, governance rules, scanning, and view derivation.
key_takeaways: ArchSpine is a documentation mirror system that ensures the atlas of documents stays synchronized with the actual codebase.
The system vision emphasizes automated governance through rules, scanners, and derived views.
Module orchestration describes how the CLI, engines, infrastructure, and services interact to provide scanning, checking, fixing, and viewing capabilities.
Maintenance boundaries are defined by the atlas structure, rule schemas, and runtime execution profiles.
